terms.definition: Evaluation protocol splitting n samples into k disjoint folds; each fold serves once as validation while the model trains on the remaining k−1 folds. Canonical definition in this topic.
k-fold CV where fold assignment preserves global class proportions in each fold—standard for imbalanced classification evaluation. Canonical definition in this topic.
Special case of k-fold with k = n: each iteration holds out one sample for validation and trains on the other n−1.
Runs k-fold CV multiple times with different random partitions (or shuffles) and averages all fold scores to reduce partition noise.
Outer CV loop estimates generalization while an inner CV loop on each outer training set performs hyperparameter or model selection.
Typically the mean of per-fold validation metrics from a cross-validation run; std across folds indicates stability.
The validation partition in one CV iteration—the only slice used for scoring that round; must not contribute to fit or preprocessing statistics.
